function.name: available_functions
function.description: Get a formatted list of all available heart use case functions.

function.name: age_group_performance
function.description: Return performance metrics for different age groups in the dataset.

function.name: count_patients
function.description: Count patients in the heart dataset. Use this when the user asks how many patients there are in total, how many patients are predicted to have heart disease, or how many patients are predicted to not have heart disease. For questions like 'how many patients have heart disease', use count_type="positive_predicted".

count_type.description: Which patient count to return. Use "total" for all patients in the dataset, "positive_predicted" for patients the model predicts as heart disease present, or "negative_predicted" for patients the model predicts as no heart disease.

function.name: show_ids
function.description: Show the IDs of all data samples in the dataset. Can be used to list IDs of all data samples in the dataset.

function.name: show_one
function.description: Show a data sample identified by an ID. Presents the original data points. Can be used to display a sample from the dataset.

patient_id.description: The id of the single data sample requested

function.name: confusion_matrix_stats
function.description: Return the confusion matrix for the model predictions, including true positives, false positives, etc.

function.name: counterfactual
function.description: Generate counterfactual examples for a patient that flip the prediction to a desired class.

patient_id.description: The ID of the patient to explain.

function.name: dataset_summary
function.description: Return one table with overall dataset statistics for each feature, similar to a dataset describe view, and optionally include one patient's feature values.

patient_id.description: Optional. ID (row index) of the patient to show alongside the overall dataset statistics.

function.name: define_feature
function.description: Return the definition of a dataset feature from metadata, including description, unit, aliases, and categorical options. Use this for questions like what a feature means or what categories it has, for example blood pressure or chest pain types.

feature.description: Feature to define. Choose one of the supported heart dataset features from this enum.

function.name: feature_importance_patient
function.description: Compute SHAP-based feature importance for a specific patient.

patient_id.description: Required ID of the patient to compute feature importance for.

function.name: feature_importance_global
function.description: Compute SHAP-based feature importance globally for the entire dataset.

function.name: feature_interactions
function.description: Return the top correlated feature pairs in the dataset.

function.name: get_dataset_description
function.description: Return a concise description of the dataset, including metadata, dataset size, number of features, target variable, and class balance in the loaded split.

function.name: get_model_description
function.description: Returns a general description of the model architecture and its purpose.

function.name: get_model_parameters
function.description: Returns the exact training hyperparameters of the model such as max_depth, criterion, and others.

function.name: misclassified_cases
function.description: Return statistics about misclassified samples and their common feature patterns.

function.name: performance_metrics
function.description: Returns selected performance metrics such as model accuracy precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC-ROC. If no parameters are provided, returns all available metrics.

metrics.description: List of metrics to return. Available options: accuracy, precision, recall, f1_score, auc_roc.

function.name: predict
function.description: Predict the class and probability scores for a specific patient using their ID.

patient_id.description: The ID of the patient to predict.

function.name: what_if
function.description: Simulate how changing a specific feature value would affect the prediction for a patient.

patient_id.description: The ID of the patient to simulate.

feature.description: The feature to modify. You may use the internal feature name, display name, or a user-facing alias such as "Chest Pain Type" or "chest pain".

value_change.description: For continuous features, the numeric value to add or subtract. For categorical features, the desired category label or code as a string, for example "Typical angina" or "1".
